# ADR-NNN: Title

<!-- IMMUTABLE: Do not edit the body of this ADR once it is written.
     Corrections and superseding decisions go into a NEW, higher-numbered ADR.
     Only the two header fields below may be updated, and the Follow-up ADRs
     field only ever gains references — it never loses them. -->

**Creation Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
**Follow-up ADRs:** —

---

## Context

Why this decision was needed. The forces at play, the constraints that made this
non-trivial, and the specific question that required a deliberate choice. If this
ADR revises or supersedes an earlier one, name it here. Write in self-contained
terms — a reader should understand the decision without any other document.

---

## Decision

The choice that was made, stated plainly. One or two sentences.

---

## Rationale

Why this option over the alternatives. Lead with the core insight. State scope
assumptions as first-principles constraints (e.g. "sufficient for a single
deployment target; revisit when a second is added") rather than as references to
external planning documents, so the rationale stays readable after plans change.

---

## Alternatives Considered

| Alternative | Reason not chosen |
|---|---|
| Option A | … |
| Option B | … |

---

## Consequences

**Positive:** what this decision enables or simplifies.

**Negative / open:** known trade-offs, deferred problems, or the conditions under
which this decision should be revisited (and thereby superseded by a follow-up ADR).
